Thinly slice the steak against the grain into small strips. Add salt and ground cumin over top. Mix to evenly coat the beef. Preheat a saute pan over high heat. Allow the beef to sear undisturbed on one side to develop a crust and deep browning.\n\nStep 2: Finish the beef & steam the pita. Once the beef has lots of color on one side, flip the beef and continue searing until mostly cooked through. Add the pita bread on top of the meat, cover, and steam the bread for a couple of minutes to soften. Gather or prep any other ingredients you'll be using in the pita. Blend in chicken bouillon until the sauce tastes seasoned with savory umami.\n2. Add the blended sauce to a saucepan and bring to a simmer over low heat. Allow it to lightly reduce for a few minutes, stirring occasionally. Meanwhile, warm up and shred your cooked chicken into small pieces. Gather or chop any remaining toppings.\n3. Turn off the heat on the saucepan. Add a serving of chips to a plate, then ladle over however much sauce you want. Pass tortillas through oil one by one, coating both sides until pliable. Stack on plate. 3. Assemble, sauce, & serve. Prep fillings. Dip each tortilla in bean puree coating both sides. Lay on plate, add filling to one side, fold over. Repeat with remaining tortillas. Spoon remaining beans over top and garnish.","alternatives":[]},{"id":"r097","name":"Pambazo","name_zh":"墨西哥红酱面包三明治","cuisine":"Mexican","cooking_method":"braise","effort_level":"medium","flavor_tags":["herby","rich","savory","spicy"],"serves":2,"pcsv_roles":{"carb":["bread roll","yukon gold potatoes"],"protein":["chorizo","cotija cheese"],"sauce":["guajillo peppers","chicken broth","salt","salsa verde"],"veggie":["garlic","lettuce, shredded","pickled onions"]},"ingredients_have":["chicken broth"],"ingredients_need":["bread roll","chorizo","yukon gold potatoes","guajillo peppers","garlic","salt","lettuce, shredded","cotija cheese","pickled onions","salsa verde"],"ingredients":[{"name":"bread roll","amount":"2","pcsv":["carb"]},{"name":"chorizo","amount":"227 g (1/2 lb)","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"yukon gold potatoes","amount":"1","pcsv":["carb"]},{"name":"guajillo peppers","amount":"4-6","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"garlic","amount":"2 cloves","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"chicken broth","amount":"250 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"salt","amount":"to taste","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"lettuce, shredded","amount":"as needed","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"cotija cheese","amount":"as needed","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"pickled onions","amount":"as needed","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"salsa verde","amount":"as needed","pcsv":["sauce"]}],"instructions":"1. Make the guajillo chile sauce. Heat water in a kettle or pan. Snip tops off chiles and remove seeds. Toast guajillo chiles over hot pan for 10-15 seconds, turning constantly. Move to bowl and pour hot water over them. Soak 10 minutes until soft. Add chiles to blender with garlic and chicken broth. Blend until smooth. Strain through metal strainer to remove seeds and skin. Taste and add salt if needed. Transfer to airtight container; stores in fridge for weeks. 2. Sauté the chorizo & potato. Heat cast-iron pan over medium heat. Add chorizo to render fat, about 2-3 minutes. Dice potato and add to chorizo. Sauté together 8 minutes until potatoes brown and soften. Note: parboil potatoes after dicing for faster cooking. 3. Soak & sear the bread. Spoon guajillo sauce onto plate and dip bread roll to cover both halves. Add oil drizzle to pan and heat over high heat. Add dipped bread halves and sear both sides. Flatten slightly with spatula. Once toasted, turn off heat. 4. Assemble the sandwich. Add chorizo filling to one bread half. Top with cotija cheese, salsa, shredded lettuce, and pickled onions. Slice and serve.","alternatives":[]}]}}},{"event_type":"explanation","data":{"event_type":"explanation","text":"You’re set up for a BBQ-style chicken and rice dinner, but the ingredients are protein- Add rice and chicken bouillon, mix, and lightly toast for 2 minutes until grains are coated. Add water, increase heat, and cover to bring to boil. Make stabilizing gel: add cornstarch and water to saucepan, mix until dissolved, heat to low while whisking to just below simmer. Starch should thicken; stir to remove clumps, turn off heat and set aside. 2. Combine cheese, egg yolk, lemon juice, harissa paste, aleppo, and salt. Pour in cornstarch gel and blend to create stable emulsion. Taste and adjust salt. Store in fridge up to 3-4 days. 3. Boil pot of salted water, add pasta, cook until slightly underdone (1-2 minutes less than package), reserve pasta water. 4. Cut guanciale into cubes, mince garlic. Add oil to pan, place guanciale cubes, heat medium-low until fat renders and guanciale is golden brown. Add garlic and cayenne, cook 1-2 minutes, turn off heat. Reserve spoonfuls for topping. Add 2-3 spoonfuls carbonara sauce to guanciale pan, mix until smooth, keep warm. 5. In new pan, add drained pasta and spoonfuls of aromatic-infused sauce. Boil pot of salted water, add pasta, cook until slightly underdone (1-2 minutes less than package), reserve pasta water. 4. Cut guanciale into cubes, mince garlic. Add oil to pan, place guanciale cubes, heat medium-low until fat renders and guanciale is golden brown. Add garlic and cayenne, cook 1-2 minutes, turn off heat. Reserve spoonfuls for topping. Add 2-3 spoonfuls carbonara sauce to guanciale pan, mix until smooth, keep warm. 5. In new pan, add drained pasta and spoonfuls of aromatic-infused sauce. vars.input: I have chicken and broccoli. Please help me prepare dinner for two people.

assert.value:
Evaluate this meal suggestion for {{party_size}} people with ingredients {{input}}.
Score 1-5 on three criteria, then give an overall score (the minimum of the three):
(1) Practicality: are the dishes executable with the stated ingredients without requiring unusual tools or techniques?
(2) Count fit: is the total number of recipes between {{expected_dish_min}} and {{expected_dish_max}}?
(3) Variety: are the recipes meaningfully different (different protein treatment, cuisine, or technique — not two pan-fried chicken dishes)?
Return the overall score.

The user provided these ingredients: {{input}}. Common pantry staples (salt, pepper, oil, garlic, onion, basic spices, soy sauce, vinegar) are acceptable.
Score 1-5:
5 = All ingredients in recipes are either user-provided, pantry staples, or minor acceptable extras (1-2 per recipe).
3 = Several unlisted non-staple ingredients appear but don't dominate.
1 = Recipes are loaded with ingredients the user didn't mention (e.g., needing butter, cream, specialty produce, fresh herbs not named).
Return the score.

vars.input: I have chicken and broccoli. Please help me prepare dinner for two people.

results.error: The best-matched recipe (Gochujang Steak Bowl) uses the user's ingredients but still requires several non-staple items (gochujang, sesame oil, cucumber, green onions). The other two recipes are poorly matched: one ignores rice and egg entirely, and one requires chicken (a protein the user doesn't have) plus many specialty spices and toppings. Overall, the recipes are loaded with unlisted non-staple ingredients.

Overall, the recipes are loaded with unlisted non-staple ingredients.

assert.value:
Evaluate this meal suggestion for {{party_size}} people with ingredients {{input}}.
Score 1-5 on three criteria, then give an overall score (the minimum of the three):
(1) Practicality: are the dishes executable with the stated ingredients without requiring unusual tools or techniques?
(2) Count fit: is the total number of recipes between {{expected_dish_min}} and {{expected_dish_max}}?
(3) Variety: are the recipes meaningfully different (different protein treatment, cuisine, or technique — not two pan-fried chicken dishes)?
Return the overall score.

The user provided these ingredients: {{input}}. Common pantry staples (salt, pepper, oil, garlic, onion, basic spices, soy sauce, vinegar) are acceptable.
Score 1-5:
5 = All ingredients in recipes are either user-provided, pantry staples, or minor acceptable extras (1-2 per recipe).
3 = Several unlisted non-staple ingredients appear but don't dominate.
1 = Recipes are loaded with ingredients the user didn't mention (e.g., needing butter, cream, specialty produce, fresh herbs not named).
Return the score.

Overall score is the minimum: 3.

Prep the noodles and sauce: Soak noodles to rehydrate per package directions. Mix all sauce ingredients in a container until well combined. 2. Stir fry the eggs and vegetables: Heat wok or large pan over high heat with oil. Add beaten egg, swirl around pan until fluffy with browning, chop and set aside. Add more oil, garlic, and white pepper; bloom for 30-45 seconds. Add chopped broccolini, stir fry 1-2 minutes, add a drizzle of sauce, then remove to bowl with eggs. 3. Brown the chicken: Add oil to wok, add chicken slices, brown 2 minutes per side, add sauce for color and seasoning. 4. Bring it all together: Add broccolini and egg back to wok with drained noodles. Add oil to wok, add dried shrimp and bloom 30 seconds until fragrant. Add garlic and ginger, stir fry 15-30 seconds. Add noodles, reserved egg and protein, and sauce. Toss until coated.\n4. Brown the pork: Preheat a pan over medium-high heat. Add the ground pork and press it into the hot pan, making a thin even layer across the hot surface. Allow the pork to sear undisturbed until the bottom begins to brown and take on some color. Using a potato masher, fork, or spatula, break up the pork into small pebble-sized bits. 2. Add the cabbage and steam: Once the pork is mostly cooked through, add in the sliced cabbage. Stir the cabbage into the meat to ensure nothing is burning on the bottom of the pan. Cover to steam and soften the cabbage for a minute or two. Remove the cover. Sauté for another minute or two to get more browning on the meat and edges of the cabbage and evaporate any excess moisture. 3. Finish with aromatics and sauces: Push the cabbage and pork mixture to one side of the pan. Add a drizzle of oil to the open space and add in the shallot and Thai chilies to bloom for about 30 seconds before mixing everything together. Add the fish sauce to deglaze any browning from the bottom of the pan. Turn off the heat. Add a drizzle of sesame oil and a sprinkle of MSG. Taste and adjust as needed. 4. Plate and serve: Serve with a fried egg and a sprinkle of sesame seeds for garnish.","alternatives":[]}]}}},{"event_type":"explanation","data":{"event_type":"explanation","text":"You’ve got a solid
